title: Pleasingly Dense Thai Suet Biryani
description: A malty, dense, and festive Thai dish featuring suet, this diabetic-friendly recipe is perfect for special occasions. It combines the richness of suet with the freshness of Thai flavors.
instructions: Heat the suet in a large saucepan over medium heat until it melts and turns into a clear oil.
Add the jasmine rice to the saucepan and stir to coat the rice with the suet oil.
Add the coconut milk, fish sauce, lime juice, and palm sugar to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the saucepan, and simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Submerge the saucepan in an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Stir in the chopped Thai basil and sliced green chilies.
Let the biryani rest for 10 minutes before serving.
Fluff the biryani with a fork and serve hot.
